title: IS-Sahel Attacks Niger-Benin Oil Pipeline in Western Niger
detail: Islamic State Sahel Province (IS-Sahel) attacked and sabotaged a section of the Niger-Benin international oil pipeline infrastructure in western Niger. The attack, part of IS-Sahel's sustained campaign against economic infrastructure in western Niger's Tillabéri and surrounding regions, was documented in a comprehensive summary of Sahel fighting from March 21–April 4. The Niger-Benin pipeline, which began exports through the West African Pipeline to the Benin coast at Seme-Kpodji, has been repeatedly targeted by armed groups seeking to undermine the Niger junta's oil revenue — a key pillar of the CNSP government's economic strategy following Western sanctions and aid suspensions after the July 2023 coup. The attack compounds the junta's fiscal pressures and highlights IS-Sahel's expanding operational scope beyond traditional armed patrols and military convoys to economic infrastructure.

sources.name: Pravda Burkina Faso — Summary of Sahel fighting March 21–April 3, April 4, 2026

media.caption: IS-Sahel Attacks Niger-Benin Oil Pipeline in Western Niger
media.source: Pravda Burkina Faso — Summary of Sahel fighting March 21–April 3, April 4, 2026

title: Boko Haram Demands 500M CFA per Hostage for Kidnapped Chadian Nationals
detail: Boko Haram issued ransom demands of up to 500 million CFA francs (approximately $900,000 USD) per surviving hostage for the six remaining Chadian nationals kidnapped in Niger's border region (one was killed on or around April 3). The demand was confirmed through reporting by Vanguard Nigeria and includes a physician among the hostages. The ransom demand illustrates Boko Haram's continued use of kidnapping as both a revenue-generating tool and a coercive tactic against neighboring states. Chad has experienced repeated cross-border incursions from Boko Haram/ISWAP elements in the Lake Chad basin, and the kidnapping of Chadian professionals in Niger intensifies pressure on N'Djamena's security services and the broader MNJTF counter-terrorism framework. No timeline for release was provided.

sources.name: Vanguard — Boko Haram demands ransoms for surviving Chadian hostages
